input.prompt: Extract the full guidelines for the "Sign in with Google" button: exact colors (hex codes) for light/dark/neutral themes, button height/padding/corner radius, font (family, weight, size), the Google "G" logo asset requirements, minimum sizes, clear space, acceptable button text variants ("Sign in with Google" vs "Sign up with Google" etc.), and any provided HTML/CSS/SVG code samples.

result.output:
# Sign in with Google Button Guidelines Summary

## Color Specifications

**Light Theme:**
- Fill: #FFFFFF
- Stroke: #747775 (1px, inside)
- Text: #1F1F1F (Google Sans Medium, 14/20)

**Dark Theme:**
- Fill: #131314
- Stroke: #8E918F (1px, inside)
- Text: #E3E3E3 (Google Sans Medium, 14/20)

**Neutral Theme:**
- Fill: #F2F2F2
- No stroke
- Text: #1F1F1F (Google Sans Medium, 14/20)

## Typography & Logo

The documentation specifies that "the button font is Google Sans Medium," available via Google Fonts. The Google "G" logo must maintain "the standard color version (the standard color gradient super G logo) and appear on a white background."

## Padding Requirements

Android & Web desktop/mobile: 12px left (before logo), 10px right (after logo), 12px right (after text)

iOS: 16px left, 12px right (after logo), 16px right (after text)

## Acceptable Button Text

Recommended call-to-action phrases include "Sign in with Google," "Sign up with Google," or "Continue with Google." The document emphasizes that localization is "permitted and encouraged."

## Key Requirements

- Buttons must preserve aspect ratio when scaled
- "The Sign in with Google button should be displayed at least as prominently as other third party sign-in options"
- The Google "G" cannot be modified in size or color
- Google Identity Services SDKs are the recommended implementation method

**Note:** The page references downloadable SVG/PNG assets but does not provide embedded CSS or HTML code samples in the extracted content.

input.prompt: Find and return verbatim any embedded HTML, CSS, or SVG code samples shown on this page for building a custom "Sign in with Google" button, including the SVG path data for the Google "G" logo mark, and exact button height in pixels, border-radius, and font-size/line-height values.

result.output:
# Sign in with Google Button Code Sample Analysis

After reviewing the provided documentation, I found **no embedded HTML, CSS, or SVG code samples** with specific technical implementations for building a custom button.

The page references:
- "An HTML configurator that allows you to customize the appearance" but provides no actual code
- Pre-approved brand icons available for download as PNG/SVG files (link provided)
- Design specifications in image format only (padding, colors, fonts)

**Key specifications mentioned (text-based only):**
- Button colors: Fill #FFFFFF (light), #131314 (dark), #F2F2F2 (neutral)
- Font: "Google Sans Medium | 14/20"
- Padding: "12px left, 10px right" (Android/Web) or "16px left, 12px right" (iOS)
- Logo requirement: "standard color version (the standard color gradient super G logo)"

**Missing from this page:**
- SVG path data for the Google "G" logo
- Exact pixel height values for buttons
- border-radius CSS values
- Working HTML/CSS code snippets

The page directs users to download assets or use the Google Identity Services SDKs rather than providing inline code examples.

Based on my web search results, here's what I found regarding the "Sign in with Google" button specifications:

## Official Button Specifications

Google provides an HTML configurator that allows you to customize the appearance of the Sign in with Google button, and you can download an HTML and CSS snippet that will render the button on your website.

### Font
The button font is Google Sans Medium. To install, first download the Google Sans font and extract the download bundle.

### Height
Based on the search results, a common implementation specifies a height of 40px for the button, though this appears to be a standard implementation rather than an officially mandated height. The official Google documentation doesn't specify a fixed height—rather, you can scale the button as needed for different devices and screen sizes, but you must preserve the aspect ratio so that the Google logo is not stretched.

### Border Radius
The official Google branding guidelines don't specify a border-radius of 40px. The examples I found show varying border-radius values (from 1-5px), with some implementations using a border-radius of 5px.

### Official Resources
Google provides an HTML configurator that allows you to customize the appearance of the Sign in with Google button, and you can download an HTML and CSS snippet that will render the button on your website. For the most accurate and up-to-date specifications, refer to the [Google Identity Branding Guidelines](https://developers.google.com/identity/branding-guidelines).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

result.output:
# CSS Values for Custom Google Sign-In Button

Based on the documentation provided, here are the exact CSS values specified:

**Button Dimensions:**
- Height: `50px` (in the `signin2.render()` example)
- Width: `240px` (example value)

**Border & Styling:**
- `border-radius: 5px`
- `border: thin solid #888`
- `box-shadow: 1px 1px 1px grey`

**Typography:**
- `font-size: 14px`
- `font-weight: bold`
- `font-family: 'Roboto', sans-serif`

**Spacing:**
- `padding-left: 42px`
- `padding-right: 42px`

**Icon/Logo:**
- Width: `42px`
- Height: `42px`

**Colors:**
- Background (normal): `white`
- Text (normal): `#444`
- Hover state: `cursor: pointer` (interaction indicator)

**Note:** The documentation does not provide specific values for pressed or disabled states, nor does it specify separate hover colors beyond the cursor change. The branding guidelines are referenced for proper color usage but exact values aren't detailed in this excerpt.

result.output:
I searched the page content for the specified terms related to button dimensions and styling. However, the document does not contain explicit measurements for corner radius, border-radius, height in pixels (20px, 40px, 48px), or detailed specifications comparing rectangular versus pill-shaped buttons.

The guidelines mention that buttons are available in "standard and icon modes" with style options including "Theme : Light, Neutral, Dark" and "Shape : Rectangular, Pill," but no pixel-specific dimensions are provided for these shapes.

The only concrete measurement given is for padding: Android & Web padding shows "12px left padding before the Google logo, 10px right padding after the Google logo and 12px right padding after the Sign in with Google text," while iOS specifies "16px left padding before the Google logo, 12px right padding after the Google logo and 16px right padding after the Sign in with Google text."

The document recommends using "Google Identity Services SDKs" which "render a Sign in with Google button that always adheres to the most recent Google branding guidelines" rather than providing manual pixel specifications for custom implementations.

content.text: Now let's run the finalisation checklist.

result.output: Launching skill: finalise

content.text:
# Run the project's mandatory finalisation checklist before reporting any task as done

## Steps

1. Run `npm run typecheck` and capture all output.
2. Run `npm run lint` and capture all output.
3. Run `npx knip` and capture all output.
4. If any of the three produced errors or warnings:
   - For lint issues — run `npm run lint:fix` first to auto-fix what it can, then re-run `npm run lint` to see what remains.
   - For knip findings — remove the unused files, exports, or dependencies it reports.
   - Fix **every** remaining issue — do not skip, suppress, or dismiss any output.
   - If you are unsure whether a problem existed before your changes, run `git stash`, repeat all three commands to establish a pre-change baseline, then `git stash pop` and fix only the new issues.
   - After fixing, re-run all three commands to confirm they are clean.
5. Repeat until all three commands exit with code 0 and report no errors or warnings.

## Done criterion

`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npx knip` all exit with code 0 and report no errors or warnings. Only then report the task as complete.

## Notes

- Fix issues in the order they appear — later errors are often caused by earlier ones.
- Avoid silencing a lint rule or adding a `@ts-ignore` / `eslint-disable` comment to make the output go away — fix the underlying problem. If a suppression is truly necessary (false positive, required vendor API), scope it as narrowly as possible (single line, specific rule) and add a short inline comment explaining why.
- If a typecheck error is in a generated file (`prisma/generated/`, `prisma/zod/`), do not edit it — regenerate it with `npx prisma generate` instead.

content.text:
All 10 tests pass, typecheck/lint/knip are clean.

**Summary of changes:**
- Added [GoogleLogo.tsx](src/components/SignIn/GoogleLogo.tsx) — the official full-colour Google "G" mark as an inline SVG (required by the guidelines; a mono icon isn't permitted).
- Updated the Google button in [SignIn.tsx](src/components/SignIn/SignIn.tsx#L130-L147) to match Google's ["Sign in with Google" branding guidelines](https://developers.google.com/identity/branding-guidelines): light-theme colours (`#FFFFFF` fill, `#747775` 1px border, `#1F1F1F` text), Google Sans Medium 14/20 type (with Roboto/Arial fallback since Google Sans isn't a licensed public webfont), and the exact specified padding (12px before the logo, 10px between logo and text, 12px after the text). Kept "Sign in with Google" as the button text — one of Google's recommended CTA phrases — and left the existing `signInWithGoogle` click handler untouched.

One judgement call: Google's guidelines permit either a Rectangular or Pill shape but don't mandate a fixed corner radius or button height, so I used a conservative 4px radius and 40px height consistent with common official button assets — flag if you'd prefer the pill style instead.
I'd prefer to use the ready-to-use SVG images

input.prompt: List every downloadable asset link on this page (icons, logos, button images, zip files) with their exact URLs, especially any links for downloading the Google "G" logo as SVG/PNG, or pre-built "Sign in with Google" button images/assets in light/dark/neutral themes.

result.output:
# Downloadable Assets from Sign in with Google Branding Guidelines

Based on the page content, here is the downloadable asset available:

## Pre-Approved Brand Icons Package

**Direct Download Link:**
- `/static/identity/images/signin-assets.zip`

**Contents:** This ZIP file contains pre-approved Sign in with Google buttons in PNG and SVG formats across multiple variations:
- **Themes:** Light, Neutral, Dark
- **Shapes:** Rectangular, Pill
- **Modes:** Standard and icon modes

**Font Requirement:** The SVG files require the Google Sans font, which can be downloaded from Google Fonts.

---

## Additional Resources Mentioned

While not directly downloadable from this page, the document references:
- **Google Sans Font:** Available at `fonts.google.com/specimen/Google+Sans` (requires separate download)
- **HTML Button Configurator:** Allows customization and HTML/CSS snippet generation (no direct asset link provided)

**Note:** The page does not provide direct SVG/PNG links for individual button variants or the Google "G" logo in isolation. Developers must download the complete `signin-assets.zip` package to access all branded button variations and assets.

questions.question: Google's asset kit provides two kinds of ready-made SVGs: a full pre-rendered button (background, border, G mark and "Sign in with Google" text all baked into one fixed-size 180×40 image) or a G-mark-only icon badge (white square/pill with border, just the logo, no text). Which should I use?

options.label: Icon-only G badge (Recommended)
options.description: Drop the official icon-only SVG in as the button's leftSection, but keep our own Mantine Button for the outer shape/border and real "Sign in with Google" text. Matches the full-width Microsoft/submit buttons already in the form, stays localizable and themeable, and avoids nesting two white bordered boxes.

options.label: Full pre-built button image
options.description: Use Google's complete 180×40 button SVG (text baked in as vector paths) as-is, wrapped in an accessible clickable element. Guarantees pixel-exact official styling but is a fixed width/height that won't match the full-width Microsoft/submit buttons, and the button text becomes an image (needs an aria-label instead of real text).

result.output: Your questions have been answered: "Google's asset kit provides two kinds of ready-made SVGs: a full pre-rendered button (background, border, G mark and "Sign in with Google" text all baked into one fixed-size 180×40 image) or a G-mark-only icon badge (white square/pill with border, just the logo, no text). Which should I use?"="Full pre-built button image". You can now continue with these answers in mind.

content.text:
**Summary:**
- `public/google-signin-button-light.svg`, `-dark.svg`, and `-neutral.svg` — Google's official pre-built "Sign in with Google" button assets (Square shape, "Show text=Yes"), unmodified, straight from their branding-guidelines asset kit.
- [SignIn.tsx](src/components/SignIn/SignIn.tsx#L131-L135) now renders the light variant via `<UnstyledButton>` wrapping a Mantine `<Image>`, with `alt="Sign in with Google"` giving it the accessible name the tests expect. `w="fit-content"` stops the Stack's `align: stretch` from pulling it to full width.
- Deleted the hand-coded `GoogleLogo.tsx` — no longer needed.

Only the light asset is wired up for now, since the app currently has no colour-scheme toggle (`defaultColorScheme="light"` with nothing to switch it). When you add theme switching, swapping in `google-signin-button-dark.svg` / `-neutral.svg` based on `useComputedColorScheme` is a small follow-up. Typecheck, lint, knip, and the SignIn test suite are all clean.
